message:
feat(python): numpy.ma.masked_outside wrapper parity (franken_numpy-ulcr)

Add fnp_python.masked_outside(x, v1, v2, copy=True) as a passthrough
delegator to np.ma.masked_outside — the complement of masked_inside.
Values strictly outside [min(v1,v2), max(v1,v2)] are masked, with
copy-flag forwarding and MaskedArray return-type contract preserved
across integer/float/2-D inputs.

Covered by one pyo3 oracle test with seven scenarios mirroring the
masked_inside test layout: float v1<v2 basic interval, reversed
v1>v2 (must normalize), bounds covering all data (no mask), bounds
outside data range (full mask), integer dtype, 2-D data, and
copy=False forwarded — each repr-compared to np.ma.masked_outside.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
